signals.title: USD/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Surge
signals.text: USD outperformance accelerates as US 10Y yields jump to 4.659% (+0.78%), driving a 1.555% G8 spread against laggard AUD. Despite a -60bp carry disadvantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), USD dominance is fueled by the 'Warsh Trade' and hawkish US fiscal/monetary expectations. AUD/USD at 0.7118 faces sustained pressure as the bear steepener regime favors the greenback over high-beta commodity FX. Watch for a break of 0.7100 to confirm further downside extension.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.814 remains below the 160.00 threshold as Japan's Finance Minister Katayama issues a direct warning of 'decisive action.' Options markets are aggressively pricing downside protection with a strong 25d RR put skew of -1.52, signaling institutional fear of a BoJ-led reversal despite the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage. JPY COT LF net short -70,605 (33.1% long) reflects a crowded carry trade vulnerable to verbal or physical intervention. Watch 160.00 for immediate BoJ response.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5843 exhibits a sharp positioning conflict: retail traders are 85% long (extreme contrarian short signal) while COT LF specs remain net short -19,437 (22.3% long). This retail-led long bias diverges from the institutional short, increasing the risk of a capitulation flush lower if the RBNZ (2.25%) fails to deliver a sufficiently hawkish hike signal on 27 May. The +150bp USD carry advantage continues to weigh on the pair. Watch for retail long liquidation below 0.5800.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Volatility Premium + Jobs Data
signals.text: GBP/USD IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 7.6% generates a +3.4% Vol Risk Premium, signaling elevated hedging demand as the UK jobs market shows signs of cracking. Despite a neutral 0bp carry spread (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%) and a net long COT position (+40,980), the 25d RR at -1.10 indicates the options market is paying for downside protection. Price at 1.34110 is vulnerable to a long-unwind if US yields continue to climb. Watch for a break of 1.3400 to trigger further spec liquidation.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Technical Breakdown + Carry
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.16140 is testing its lowest levels since April 8th as sellers retain control following a failure to sustain early session gains. The +175bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%) remains the structural anchor for the move, while COT positioning shows specs are already net short -8,151. With VIX at 17.8 (normal volatility), the carry-driven divergence is the primary driver. Watch for a sustained break below 1.1593 to confirm a move toward 1.1500.
